feedback.text: Dear, I have been an aaat Gamer for a good moment, seeing many games inducing extreme ‘Made ha for and become strong’ items in many ingame shops. Like ‘Timegates’ in touch (example, 1 day for and lock in a Danish), but paying now, you can cast it now. Many games on PC og PS5 use Mobil’s eager to spend MYCKET more than humane, often you get out badly for several weeks afterwards when you spend too much. Ingame currency is also often unable to get a refund! This may be done with you (for example, you know that there are many videos on people who have used – Swiped – up to 100.000 seconds. — USD 10.000 on Black Desert Online to become ‘strongest’ in PVP) As you should also see, Games, who are being augmented after that, have said that they will no longer serve them and take down servers, so you can never play these games more. This is wrong, nobody should be able to lose games they have flown! A digital game is ‘Gods’ and not something they can suddenly withdraw from you. Imagine if you had flown something at the shop, let’s say an IPAD, then suddenly, the shop and kick on your door, saying “sorry but you don’t own the IPADEN, we must have it back because we will stop updating it and it will finish functioning” You may cast something with “Games as a Service” for this is not correct, it is a “Gods” like you kjöper, icke a service. They deceive you to believe that you’re a ‘Service’, but you are paying for a ‘License’ for a lifetime. Many games studios want to teach you to float a game like the Live Service, but it is really a lifetime Liicence, then they take the game to you when they talk a day that they will not continue the updaters and will terminate it! This is not correct, games should be a good you own, not something you rent until they say they stop the game’s lifetime. They must have an opportunity to enable customers to continue and play the games they believe. You can also see more info about this on the “Stop Killing Games initiative” where we received 1.4 millions of signatures to stop producers to kill games we have paid for. It is a Costumers Right Violation. I hope you will be able to include this in your new points. —End dead games, the right to own a game is not with the customer – the producer does not put more blocks on microtransactions, so they cannot ruinate life to as many people that depend on games. (this is very serious in many players)
